q: The electromotive force is actually a force.
A source of electromotive force has an internal resistance.
The internal resistance of a battery depends on its size.
The internal resistance of a battery does not depend on its temperature.
Work has to be done in moving a unit positive charge through the internal resistor.
A fuse is made of a thick short wire of tin-lead alloy.
A potentiometer can be used to measure the emf of a cell.
The resistance of a conductor is directly proportional to its length.
Mica cannot withstand very high temperatures.
The voltage of a simple light bulb used in Myanmar is 220 V.
An electric iron has an electrical power of 750-1000 W.
A thermostat switches the current on and off to maintain the selected temperature.
The internal resistance must be regarded as being connected in series to the battery.
The electrical resistivity of a material depends on its temperature.
Kirchhoff's first rule is based on the law of conservation of charge.
The terminal voltage of a battery is always equal to its emf.
Wheatstone bridge is used to measure unknown resistance.
The sum of the currents entering a junction is equal to the sum of the currents leaving it.
The internal resistance of a battery does not depend on the chemicals used in it.
Kirchhoff's second rule is based on the law of conservation of energy.
The resistivity of a semiconductor decreases with increasing temperature.
Superconductors have zero resistance below a certain critical temperature.
Electrical energy can be transformed into light energy.
The power dissipated in a resistor is P = VI.
A fuse prevents electrical appliances from being passed through by excessive current.
The heating effect of electric current is used in an electric stove.
Tungsten filament in a bulb emits white light at about 2500 °C.
The inert gas reduces the evaporation of the filament in the light bulb.
In a series circuit, the current is the same through all components.
In a parallel circuit, the potential difference is the same across all branches.
The internal resistance of a battery increases with its lifetime.
Ohmmeter is an instrument used to measure electrical resistance.
Electrical power is the rate at which electrical energy is dissipated.
The kilowatt-hour (kWh) is a unit of electrical power.
One kilowatt-hour is equal to 3.6 x 10^6 Joules.
The resistance of a wire is inversely proportional to its cross-sectional area.
Ohm's law is valid for all types of conductors under all conditions.
Ammeter should always be connected in parallel with the circuit component.
Voltmeter should always be connected in series with the circuit component.
A bridge is balanced when no current flows through the galvanometer.
Nichrome is commonly used as a heating element.
The potential difference across a resistor is V = IR.
The unit of resistivity is Ohm-metre (Ω m).
An electric light bulb produces light energy only.
A battery provides electrical power.
The fuse wire may be fitted in a glass cartridge.
Resistivity is a property of the material of the conductor.
The equivalent resistance of resistors in series is R = R1 + R2 + ...
The equivalent resistance of resistors in parallel is always greater than any individual resistance.
A galvanometer can be converted into an ammeter by connecting a low resistance in parallel.
